reasoning: The Swiss Federal Tribunal (SFT) has historically overturned CAS decisions in only about 1% of appeals. SFT does not hear appeals on the merits or accept new factual evidence (such as the new video US Gymnastics found), but only rules on very narrow procedural violations (e.g., right to be heard, jurisdiction). Since the factual dispute over the exact timing of the inquiry is the core of the USA's case, it is highly unlikely the SFT will find sufficient legal grounds to annul the CAS arbitral award. Taking into account the base rate and the strict bounds (>= 0.05), a 5% probability is the most appropriate estimate.
